Whenever there is a series: Aus vs Ind, Eng vs SA, etc., you should invite an associate nation to play a warm-up-style game against one of the competing teams, or against both. So, for instance, before New Zealand play England, we let Scotland play one or both of them. So Scotland gets a great chance to show up, and even an "upset" by an associate nation is exciting going into the series; it wouldn't diminish it, because the host nation may not play their "strongest side" or whatever. It still gives the Nepals of this world more games.

Happy as an Indian but NZ were strategically shocking today. So many tactical errors: dropping Mcconchie, not giving Henry the 3rd over, not letting Philips bowl again to Sharma, switching to slower balls so early in the game, Neesham bowling full pace to Dube in the last over despite slower balls working so well, Mitchell batting at 6, Santner coming above Neesham. I would even say bowling first in the final was a mistake too. It was bewildering from NZ.
